subcommands.name: clock
subcommands.description: Returns the current clock value for a watched root

subcommands.name: find
subcommands.description: Finds all files that match the optional list of patterns under the specified dir

subcommands.name: flush-subscriptions
subcommands.description: This is designed to be used by interactive programs that have a background process or daemon maintaining a subscription to Watchman

subcommands.name: get-config
subcommands.description: Returns the .watchmanconfig for the root

subcommands.name: path
subcommands.description: The path to root

subcommands.name: get-sockname
subcommands.description: Get socket path

subcommands.name: list-capabilities
subcommands.description: Returns the full list of supported capabilities offered by the watchman server

subcommands.name: log
subcommands.description: Generates a log line in the watchman log

subcommands.name: log-level
subcommands.description: Changes the log level of your connection to the watchman service

subcommands.name: query
subcommands.description: Executes a query against the specified root

subcommands.name: shutdown-server
subcommands.description: This causes your watchman service to exit with a normal status code

subcommands.name: since
subcommands.description: Finds all files that were modified since the specified clockspec that match the optional list of patterns

subcommands.name: state-enter
subcommands.description: This causes a watch to be marked as being in a particular named state

subcommands.name: state-leave
subcommands.description: This causes a watch to no longer be marked as being in a particular named state

subcommands.name: subscribe
subcommands.description: Subscribes to changes against a specified root and requests that they be sent to the client via its connection

subcommands.name: trigger
subcommands.description: This will create or replace a trigger

subcommands.name: trigger-del
subcommands.description: Deletes a named trigger from the list of registered triggers

subcommands.name: trigger-list
subcommands.description: Returns the set of registered triggers associated with a root directory

subcommands.name: unsubscribe
subcommands.description: Cancels a named subscription against the specified root

subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: The version and build information for the currently running watchman service

subcommands.name: watch-del
subcommands.description: Removes a watch and any associated triggers

subcommands.name: watch-del-all
subcommands.description: Removes all watches and associated triggers

subcommands.name: watch-list
subcommands.description: Returns a list of watched dirs

subcommands.name: watch-project
subcommands.description: Requests that the project containing the requested dir is watched for changes

options.description: Spawning from an inetd style supervisor
Don't use the site or system spawner
Specify alternate unix domain socket path
Specify alternate unix domain socket path
Persist and wait for further responses
Specify path to file to hold watch and trigger state
Instead of parsing CLI arguments, take a single json object from stdin
Run the service in the foreground
Don't try to start the service if it is not available
When no-spawn is enabled, don't try to handle request in client mode if service is unavailable
